H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Strong confidence tier (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, 66% positive). Clear bimodal peaks at 5 (14 responses) and 8-9 (26+17 responses) — panel converged on subgroup effect. Qualitative: 41% noticeable improvement, 10-minute settle for 38%. Music loved/liked by 73%. AutoGluon predicts 6.991 virtually matching observed. YouTube moderate 30 views, no retention data. Cross-bucket routing to X-ME protocols suggested.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Top performer for feeling overwhelmed — helps two distinct groups, some quickly and others after longer sessions. Strong music appeal.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Strong confidence (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, 65% positive). Distribution peaks at 7-8 with concentration. Qualitative: 48% improvement reports with balanced music opinions. AutoGluon 6.991 predicted vs 6.92 observed — close alignment. YouTube 5 views. Settling split between 10-minute (48%) and immediate (14%).
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Reliable overstimulation relief with balanced improvement, settling typically within 10 minutes for most listeners.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, 64% positive). Distribution shows 76% positive skew toward 7-9. Qualitative: 61% improvement with balanced/comfortable rhythm opinions (10 each). AutoGluon 6.991 predicted. YouTube 8 views. Better-than-expected for developing confidence tier.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Good overstim help with concentrated positive responses — listeners appreciate the balanced, comfortable rhythm.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Strong confidence tier (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, 63% positive). Distribution peaks at 8-9 with 26 total responses. Qualitative shows 65% improvement reports with 10-minute settle time; 82% music appreciation. AutoGluon predicts 7.934 for anxiety but recommends cross-bucket for low zone (8.311). YouTube reach minimal (5 views) — high-efficacy signal under-discovered. Panel divided on whether peak at 8-9 indicates true efficacy or ceiling artifact.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: This anxiety protocol works well for most listeners, with many feeling noticeably calmer within about 10 minutes — music gets strong appreciation.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, 67% positive). Balanced distribution concentrated at 7-8. Qualitative: mixed improvement ratings with 45% reporting noticeable change, 5% slight improvement. AutoGluon predicts 7.934 for anxiety — 0.94pt delta. YouTube 6 views. No bimodality detected but mid-range concentration suggests moderate efficacy ceiling.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: A solid stress-relief option that helps people settle at their own pace, whether during therapy, driving, or other daily activities.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, 61% positive). Distribution slightly left-skewed with response spread across 4-10. Qualitative: 40% noticeable improvement, settling near session end for 50%. AutoGluon predicts 7.934 — 0.85pt delta. YouTube 4 views. Higher std indicates response variability not seen in L1/L2.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Effective stress management with most people feeling better by the end of the session, especially those who need longer to relax.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence tier (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, 53% positive). Distribution shows 12 responses at score 8 — notable high-end concentration. Qualitative: 54% improvement in acute distress, though 8/24 report significant change. AutoGluon predicts 7.748 for crisis zone — 1.4pt delta indicating underperformance. YouTube 6 views. Crisis baseline structurally met but room for entrainment optimization.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: The foundational crisis support option that helps over half of listeners feel better — a reliable starting point for acute distress.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, 55% positive). Distribution concentrated at 7-8 with slight high-end skew. Qualitative shows 56% improvement with moderate-to-significant ratings. AutoGluon predicts 7.748 — 1.23pt shortfall. YouTube 4 views. Settling times split between 5-10 minutes, suggesting variable entrainment windows.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Grounding protocol meeting baseline expectations with a mix of immediate and gradual settling patterns across users.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, 52% positive). Distribution shows 15% at score 0-1 and 26% at 8-9 — potential early dropout effect. Qualitative reports 60% improvement but also 15% no effect. Settling: 26% immediate within 1-2 minutes. AutoGluon predicts 7.748 — 1.23pt delta. YouTube 5 views. Highest drift signal in crisis set (sim=0.778).
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Crisis option that works for some immediately but may lose others early — worth refining for faster engagement.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Strong confidence (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, 65% positive). Distribution peaks at 9 (11) with secondary peak at 5. Notable: 87% respondent type =Myself self-reporting. Qualitative: 73% sleep-specific improvement, high music appreciation. AutoGluon predicts 7.088 — 0.1pt above. YouTube severely underperforming at 7 views. DRIFT signal notable (sim=0.714).
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Excellent sleep protocol with deeper relaxation responses — seems to work especially well for people using it personally, but hasn't reached many listeners yet.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, 67% positive). Distribution concentrated at 8-9 (13+10 responses) — tight clustering. Qualitative: mixed music opinions but good rhythm acceptance. AutoGluon 7.088 predicted. YouTube 5 views. Higher listener_type diversity (6 client, 5 support) vs L1 self-report skew.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Solid sleep aid with tight clustering of positive outcomes — good for those who can commit to the full session.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, 64% positive). Low n masks potential patterns; distribution shows mild positive skew. Qualitative: 64% improvement, 5-10 minute settling. AutoGluon 7.088 predicted. YouTube 3 views — lowest in sleep series. Need data expansion for stability.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Promising sleep tool with potential, though small sample limits what we can definitively say about its effectiveness.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, 56% positive). Lowest Focus & Clarity performer. Distribution shows 12 at scores 7-8 but also high neutral cluster. Qualitative: 74% focus improvement reported — notable divergence from 56% pct_pos. Rhythm opinions split 9 slightly-busy vs 6 calming. AutoGluon predicts 7.003. YouTube 5 views. Panel flagged as underperforming versus higher subjective reports.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus protocol that users say helps them concentrate but might need tweaking to match subjective experience with outcome scores.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, 66% positive). Strong middle-tier Focus performer. Distribution skews toward 8-9 (6+10). Qualitative: 65% improvement with 5-10 minute settling. Rhythm: 8 balanced vs 5 slightly-busy. AutoGluon 7.003 predicted. YouTube 4 views. DRIFT notable (sim=0.737).
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Good clarity tool with balanced results across different activities — reliable middle-ground performer.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, 71% positive). THRIVING series leader with notable: 10 respondents at score 10. Qualitative: 76% significant improvement, 15 at 10-min settle. AutoGluon recommends for focus zone (7.003) but predicts lower than observed — possible efficacy upside. YouTube 16 views, highest in THRIVING. High std suggests variable responder subgroups.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: The standout focus protocol in the collection — most users report significant improvement in their ability to concentrate.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, 53% positive). Distribution near-normal with slight mid-range concentration. Qualitative: 62% creative flow improvement despite lower pct_pos. AutoGluon predicts 6.991 for overstim zone with cross-bucket routing to X-ME. YouTube 2 views — lowest reach in series. Settling split 6 immediate/6 at 10 min.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative flow enhancer with moderate benefits — helps about half of listeners, with varying settle times.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, 58% positive). Tightest std in Creative Flow series — consistent responses. Qualitative: 67% improvement with strong music appreciation (12 loved/liked). AutoGluon 6.991 for overstim zone. YouTube 2 views. Settling spread across 5-20 min windows.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Consistent creative support where most users enjoy the music and feel meaningful improvement in their flow states.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Developing confidence (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, 67% positive). Highest mean in Creative Flow with lowest std — strong efficacy signal despite low n. Qualitative: 72% improvement, balanced rhythm opinions. AutoGluon 7.003 predicting vs 7.28 observed — slight underprediction. YouTube 7 views. DRIFT signal (sim=0.787).
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Standout creative catalyst with high improvement rates despite smaller group — many report significant creative breakthroughs.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, 64% positive). Best std in Maintenance & Balance — consistent protocol. Distribution peaks at 7-8. Qualitative: 65% improvement with therapy session activity dominant (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.154 close to observed. YouTube 3 views. T-MB protocols cluster semantically with sim=0.954 to L3.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Good baseline maintenance option with consistent positive experiences, especially beneficial during therapy sessions.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, 59% positive). Highest variance in series with bimodal risk: 32% neutral responses (scores 3-5). Qualitative: mixed improvement reports. AutoGluon 7.154 predicted vs 6.68 observed — 0.46pt underperformance. YouTube 4 views. DRIFT signal (sim=0.763).
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Maintenance protocol showing mixed results — helps some but leaves others wanting more stabilization support.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, 71% positive). High positivity despite low n. Qualitative: 4/19 Did NOT settle — concerning. AutoGluon 7.154 predicted vs 6.93 observed. YouTube 3 views. Highest DRIFT signal overall (sim=0.703) — most analytically unstable protocol in library.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Promising balance tool that many respond well to, though a few listeners don't settle at all — needs more data to understand why.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, 54% positive). AutoGluon predicted 8.311 for low zone — 1.57pt higher than observed, strongest underperformance signal. Qualitative: 59% mood improvement with 10 Loved it music. Rhythm balanced. YouTube 8 views. Longest settle time (6 at 20 min). Cross-bucket routing to anxiety/crisis zones suggested.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood and energy tool that users say helps significantly but takes longer to work (about 20 minutes) — could benefit from faster-acting elements.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, 64% positive). Highest positive rate in Mood & Energy. Distribution concentrated 7-9. Qualitative: 60% mood improvement, significant improvement 7/15. AutoGluon predicts 8.311 — 1.55pt delta. YouTube 8 views. Settling split immediate/20 min.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Good mood enhancer with the highest satisfaction in its category — users appreciate the balanced, settling rhythm.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Developing (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, 56% positive). Lowest Mood & Energy performer. Distribution shows 20% neutral (no effect 5). Qualitative: 60% improvement but notably 5/25 no effect. AutoGluon 8.311 predicted — 2.03pt delta, largest in library. YouTube 4 views. Requires hold-phase adjustment.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mood option with mixed results — helps many but a notable portion feel no effect, suggesting inconsistent performance.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, 45% positive). Lowest positive rate in Motivation set. Distribution spread across 4-9 with notable: 4 at score 9. Qualitative: 59% motivation improvement despite low pct_pos. AutoGluon predicts 7.552 for motivation — 1.19pt delta. YouTube 5 views. Settling fast (9 at 5 min) but efficacy muted.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation protocol meeting baseline expectations for an ascending design — helps about half of users get more engaged.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, 43% positive). Lowest positive rate in library. Distribution peaks at 7-8. Qualitative: 57% improvement but 5 slight and 5 moderate only. AutoGluon 7.552 predicted vs 6.21 observed — 1.34pt delta. YouTube 7 views. Delayed settling (5 at 20 min).
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Drive tool with improvement but often with a delay — users feel the effect after about 20 minutes rather than quickly.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Developing (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, 62% positive). Outlier ASCENT performer — mean 1pt higher than L1/L2. Distribution: 3 at 9, 2 at 10. Qualitative: 94% improvement (only 17 responses) — strong signal despite n. AutoGluon 7.552 predicted vs 7.15 observed. YouTube 7 views. High std suggests bimodal potential as n expands.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Surprising outlier that performs much better than its volume mates despite small sample — exceptional improvement reports.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, 54% positive). Near-equal split positive/neutral. Qualitative: 52% mind clarity improvement with 10 Notable improvement. AutoGluon predicts 6.964 for mind-clarity zone. YouTube 8 views. Distribution shows 5 at score 8 and 5 at 9 — high-end concentration.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind clarity tool that works decently for about half of listeners, with strong musical appreciation.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Developing confidence (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, 56% positive). Music loved by 88% — highest in TRANSFORMING. Qualitative: 65% clarity improvement. AutoGluon 6.964 predicted. YouTube 12 views. Low n requires hedging; distribution nearly uniform.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Clarity option with surprisingly high music love but moderate improvement — may resonate better as it reaches more listeners.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Developing instability (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, 50% positive). Panel converged as underperforming protocol. Distribution: scores at 2,3,4,5,8,9 with high variance. Qualitative: 1 It actually made things worse rating. AutoGluon predicts 6.964 — 1.26pt delta. YouTube 8 views. Highest std in library (2.45) indicates response heterogeneity. Need fundamental revision.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Clarity protocol that needs attention — some listeners report it actually made things worse, indicating possible design issues.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The three series show differentiated efficacyProfiles with HEALING demonstrating tightest consistency while TRANSFORMING exhibits expected variance for ascending-arc design.
• HEALING leads in stability (std=1.77) with H-OS-L1 and H-SL-L1 as top performers (7.15 and 7.19 means)
• THRIVING shows peak efficacy in Focus & Clarity (T-FC-L3 mean=7.63) confirming HOLD protocol design
• AutoGluon predicts higher outcomes than observed for crisis (1.4pt delta) and mood protocols (1.5pt delta)
• Panel converged on bimodality signal for H-OS-L1 and underperformance signal for X-MC-L3
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series performance aligns with design intent while revealing protocol-level idiosyncrasies.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols demonstrate strong efficacy, with overstimulation and sleep subsets leading (means 7.04-7.19)
• THRIVING HOLD protocols show stable outcomes, particularly T-FC-L3 (71% positive, mean=7.63) exceeding predictions
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols exhibit widest spread (std=1.87), with X-MD-L3 outlier (7.15) outperforming X-MD-L1/L2
• AutoGluon feature importance ranks retention metrics (avg_view_percentage=0.439) above protocol design parameters
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols deviate from expected patterns warranting investigation.
• H-OS-L1 shows bimodal distribution with peaks at 5 and 8-9 suggesting population subgroup effect
• X-MC-L3 reports lowest mean (5.70) with highest variance (std=2.45) and developing confidence (n=10)
• Cross-bucket routing: AutoGluon recommends X-ME protocols for anxiety/crisis zones despite different series designation
• H-CR protocols show 1.2-1.4pt shortfall from predicted scores, suggesting entrainment timing may need adjustment
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Concrete protocol refinements identified from convergent analyzer signals.
• H-OS-L1 (n=95) — [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Bimodal distribution warrants Adviser routing revision to segment by listener type
• X-MC-L3 (n=10) — [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Low outcome with high variance requires ASCENT curve restructuring; increase data collection target to n=30
• T-FC-L3 (n=35) — AutoGluon predicts 7.003 vs observed 7.63; test HOLD phase extension for even higher efficacy
• X-ME-L1 (n=39) — Predicted 8.311 vs observed 6.74 signals underperformance; add calming rhythm cues and extend hold phase
• H-AX-L1 (n=67) — High efficacy (7.22) with minimal YouTube reach (5 views) indicates undiscovered strength — prioritize promotion
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding context shows evolving analytical patterns across protocols.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 16 protocols show notable drift (similarity<0.85), with T-MB-L3 (0.703), H-SL-L1 (0.714), T-FC-L2 (0.737) most unstable
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: T-MB-L1↔T-MB-L3 (0.954) and X-ME-L1↔X-ME-L3 (0.947) cluster tightly by zone design
• CR-3 appears analytically isolated (mean_sim=0.721) — review protocol framing for consistency
• Panel divided on drift interpretation: some view as evolving listener patterns, others as parameter sensitivity

by_protocol.X-MD-L3: Developing (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, 62% positive). Outlier ASCENT performer — mean 1pt higher than L1/L2. Distribution: 3 at 9, 2 at 10. Qualitative: 94% improvement (only 17 responses) — strong signal despite n. AutoGluon 7.552 predicted vs 7.15 observed. YouTube 7 views. High std suggests bimodal potential as n expands.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: Meaningful confidence (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, 54% positive). AutoGluon predicted 8.311 for low zone — 1.57pt higher than observed, strongest underperformance signal. Qualitative: 59% mood improvement with 10 Loved it music. Rhythm balanced. YouTube 8 views. Longest settle time (6 at 20 min). Cross-bucket routing to anxiety/crisis zones suggested.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: Meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, 64% positive). Highest positive rate in Mood & Energy. Distribution concentrated 7-9. Qualitative: 60% mood improvement, significant improvement 7/15. AutoGluon predicts 8.311 — 1.55pt delta. YouTube 8 views. Settling split immediate/20 min.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: Developing (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, 56% positive). Lowest Mood & Energy performer. Distribution shows 20% neutral (no effect 5). Qualitative: 60% improvement but notably 5/25 no effect. AutoGluon 8.311 predicted — 2.03pt delta, largest in library. YouTube 4 views. Requires hold-phase adjustment.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: This anxiety protocol works well for most listeners, with many feeling noticeably calmer within about 10 minutes — music gets strong appreciation.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: A solid stress-relief option that helps people settle at their own pace, whether during therapy, driving, or other daily activities.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Effective stress management with most people feeling better by the end of the session, especially those who need longer to relax.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: The foundational crisis support option that helps over half of listeners feel better — a reliable starting point for acute distress.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Grounding protocol meeting baseline expectations with a mix of immediate and gradual settling patterns across users.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Crisis option that works for some immediately but may lose others early — worth refining for faster engagement.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Top performer for feeling overwhelmed — helps two distinct groups, some quickly and others after longer sessions. Strong music appeal.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Reliable overstimulation relief with balanced improvement, settling typically within 10 minutes for most listeners.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Good overstim help with concentrated positive responses — listeners appreciate the balanced, comfortable rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Excellent sleep protocol with deeper relaxation responses — seems to work especially well for people using it personally, but hasn't reached many listeners yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Solid sleep aid with tight clustering of positive outcomes — good for those who can commit to the full session.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Promising sleep tool with potential, though small sample limits what we can definitively say about its effectiveness.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Creative flow enhancer with moderate benefits — helps about half of listeners, with varying settle times.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Consistent creative support where most users enjoy the music and feel meaningful improvement in their flow states.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Standout creative catalyst with high improvement rates despite smaller group — many report significant creative breakthroughs.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Focus protocol that users say helps them concentrate but might need tweaking to match subjective experience with outcome scores.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Good clarity tool with balanced results across different activities — reliable middle-ground performer.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: The standout focus protocol in the collection — most users report significant improvement in their ability to concentrate.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Good baseline maintenance option with consistent positive experiences, especially beneficial during therapy sessions.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Maintenance protocol showing mixed results — helps some but leaves others wanting more stabilization support.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Promising balance tool that many respond well to, though a few listeners don't settle at all — needs more data to understand why.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Mind clarity tool that works decently for about half of listeners, with strong musical appreciation.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Clarity option with surprisingly high music love but moderate improvement — may resonate better as it reaches more listeners.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Clarity protocol that needs attention — some listeners report it actually made things worse, indicating possible design issues.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Motivation protocol meeting baseline expectations for an ascending design — helps about half of users get more engaged.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Drive tool with improvement but often with a delay — users feel the effect after about 20 minutes rather than quickly.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Surprising outlier that performs much better than its volume mates despite small sample — exceptional improvement reports.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Mood and energy tool that users say helps significantly but takes longer to work (about 20 minutes) — could benefit from faster-acting elements.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Good mood enhancer with the highest satisfaction in its category — users appreciate the balanced, settling rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Mood option with mixed results — helps many but a notable portion feel no effect, suggesting inconsistent performance.
